title: Romulea sublutea
taxonomy: Romulea sublutea belongs to the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. It is a genus of flowering plants that primarily consists of geophytes found in southern Africa. Romulea sublutea is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Western Cape province in South Africa. The species is further classified within the tribe Irideae, which includes various other irises and related plants. Romulea sublutea is a geophyte that grows from a corm, which is a type of underground storage organ.

intro: Romulea sublutea, also known as the golden carpet or gold-flowered Romulea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wound healing, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Romulea sublutea include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Romulea sublutea is often prepared as a powder, extract, or tea, and is used in formulations for various health purposes, including skin care products and respiratory supplements.
